FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: 5 stories spanning regulatory headwinds, regional M&A, a major antitrust win, and fintech infrastructure moves.

bullets.text: Swedish court awards Klarna €2B in antitrust judgment against Google for preferential treatment of its own price comparison service over Prisjakt.
Warren confronts CFPB nominee Brian Johnson (ex-Capital One) on potential conflict of interest regarding dropped lawsuit against his former employer ahead of confirmation vote.
140+ consortium members including BNY Mellon, Visa, and Coinbase launch low-cost stablecoin for payments infrastructure under Bridge CEO leadership.
EagleBank pays $9.7M to resolve BSA/AML violations including admitted knowledge of customer fraud and weak compliance controls.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Vanguard outsources to T. Rowe Price, RIA consolidation continues, and AI automation gains traction amid crypto outflows and M&A disputes.

bullets.text: Vanguard taps T. Rowe Price as subadvisor on three active funds after internal quant strategies underperformed—a rare outsourcing move for the giant that signals quant headwinds despite higher fees.
Clearstead acquires $5.6B RIA team from myCIO Wealth Partners; mid-market consolidation continues as roll-up appetite remains strong.
Bitcoin ETFs logged $6.4B outflows in worst 30-day stretch since launch, while RIAs explore section 351 exchanges to convert SMAs into ETFs.
FactSet invests in TIFIN.AI and rolls out agentic AI workflows on its Workstation to automate meeting prep and portfolio intelligence for wealth managers.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Reinsurance market flooded with $790bn capital driving pricing compression, while primary insurers face outsized Q2 losses and new players target underserved SME segment.

bullets.text: Record $790bn reinsurance capital at mid-year renewals enabled double-digit pricing cuts on property catastrophe coverage—Kingstone locked in 15%+ cost reduction with expanded limits.
Goldman Sachs pegs Q2'26 global insured catastrophe losses at $24bn, with primary insurers absorbing 65–70% while reinsurers shield downside.
Fidelis Partnership launching Adroit Underwriting MGA to underwrite US SME casualty reinsurance, filling gap in mid-market risk coverage.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Sovereigns flood private credit while fintech innovates at the margins; mainstream payments players embrace stablecoins as crypto litigation looms.

bullets.text: Sovereign wealth funds rotating hard into private credit, signaling sustained capital demand for specialty finance assets.
North American private credit hitting reputational and performance headwinds while Europe remains resilient—two regions diverging on structure and regulation.
Visa, Mastercard, US Bank, Google, and Coinbase backing Open USD stablecoin launching this year—mainstream convergence on digital currency infrastructure.
1,700 UK investors suing Binance for £150M+ damages, highlighting regulatory and litigation exposure in crypto platforms.

PE & Deals.lede: PE fundraising momentum continues despite LP skepticism—top firms crush targets while some pensions reassess allocations, and distressed software assets are drawing tactical bets.

bullets.text: Colorado PERA ($75bn) questioning PE commitment after underperformance vs. benchmarks—broadening signal that LP confidence in the asset class is cracking.
Top 100 PE firms raised nearly 10% more capital YoY; KKR holds lead, Bain Capital vaults into top five—momentum defies LP concerns.
Arizona pension eyeing tactical PE boost to snag distressed software assets hit by valuation compression and debt walls from peak-multiple deals.
BCI tripled PE outlays in 2025 with fresh global PE lead signaling continued appetite for 'complex' opportunities—contrasts sharply with pullback sentiment elsewhere.

Markets & Macro.lede: 5 stories spanning PE expansion into pension buyouts, crypto regulatory upheaval, wealth transfer opportunities, private credit stress, and regulatory uncertainty from Supreme Court ruling.

bullets.text: KKR entering UK/European pension buyouts as PE firms target attractive yields from sponsors offloading longevity risk.
MiCA compliance deadline forces majority of EU crypto firms to exit; only fraction secured required licences as new rules take effect.
$84 trillion wealth transfer over 20 years creates $60 trillion AUM opportunity for asset managers, but generational handoffs historically see 70% client attrition.
Medallia's post-SPAC equity wipeout and debt distress exposes structural tensions in private credit's venture-into-growth-stage playbook.

Newsletters.lede: ETF boom hits $23T globally while SEC scrutinizes complex products; yen weakness signals Fed rate hike bets; wealth transfer headwinds from longevity.

bullets.text: Global ETF assets hit $23T in May with inflows exceeding $739B YTD—industry momentum accelerating despite SEC's push to review leveraged, inverse, and derivative-linked structures for investor protection.
SEC opening comment period on novel ETF products signals regulatory tension between innovation and stability—watch for constraints on complex strategies gaining traction with retail.
JPY hits 40-year low as Fed rate hike expectations take hold, upending yen's safe-haven bid and creating near-term currency headwinds for dollar-denominated asset managers.
$84T intergenerational wealth transfer at risk—rising healthcare costs and extended lifespans eating into boomer savings, potentially dampening near-term estate planning and advisory fee pipelines.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 4 stories spanning prediction markets, AI infrastructure economics, and labor market impacts as enterprise AI adoption accelerates despite rising costs.

bullets.text: Risk Labs' decentralized prediction-market oracle has proven exploitable despite crypto-game-theory complexity, with dozens of bettors reporting losses from manipulation.
Factory ($1.5B AI-native software company) is tackling the 'token budget problem'—LLMs hitting context limits when processing large codebases, forcing developers to trade breadth for accuracy.
Enterprise AI spending accelerates despite 600x infrastructure cost declines in six years; paradox driven by surging compute demand and architectural inefficiency, not pricing pressure.
Ramp data shows heavy AI-spending companies are hiring, not laying off—directly contradicting the automation-kills-jobs narrative dominating tech discourse.

all.lede: Five stories spanning asset management strategy shifts, regulatory scrutiny of exotic ETFs, and mid-market RIA consolidation.

bullets.text: Vanguard outsources to T. Rowe Price as subadvisor on three active funds after internal quants underperformed—rare move for the passive giant that will raise fees for clients.
Sovereign wealth funds are aggressively rotating from public equities and bonds into private credit, signaling sustained capital demand for specialty finance.
SEC is seeking public comment on novel ETF structures (leveraged, inverse, derivative-linked) to weigh innovation against systemic risks and investor protection.
Clearstead acquired $5.6B RIA team from myCIO Wealth Partners—mid-market consolidation play following the hire of a new M&A head.
